# Camera Remote Web API Documentation

> TypeScript SDK and HTTP API for controlling Sony cameras remotely.

## Instructions for LLMs

When generating code that controls Sony cameras using this SDK, follow these guidelines:

### Always use CameraManager

`CameraManager` is the recommended entry point. Do NOT use `CameraClient`, `EventStream`, `ServerManager`, or raw HTTP requests unless the user explicitly asks for stateful/manual control.

```typescript
import { CameraManager } from 'camera-remote-web-api/server';

const manager = new CameraManager({ port: 8080 });

manager.on('camera-ready', async ({ cameraId }) => {
  await manager.client.setPriorityKey(cameraId, { setting: 'pc-remote' });
  // Use manager.client for all camera operations
});

await manager.start();
```

- Import from `camera-remote-web-api/server` for Node.js (spawns and manages the native binary).
- Import from `camera-remote-web-api` for browser (connects to an already-running server).
- Access the HTTP client via `manager.client` — do not instantiate `CameraClient` separately.

### Always handle graceful shutdown

The native camera server binary must be stopped when your application exits. Always include shutdown handling:

```typescript
// Graceful shutdown — always include this
process.on('SIGINT', async () => {
  await manager.close();
  process.exit(0);
});

process.on('SIGTERM', async () => {
  await manager.close();
  process.exit(0);
});
```

`manager.close()` disconnects all cameras, closes SSE subscriptions, stops polling, and kills the native binary process. Without this, the binary may continue running as an orphaned process.

### ESM-only package

`camera-remote-web-api` is ESM-only. Projects must have `"type": "module"` in `package.json`:

```bash
npm init -y
npm pkg set type=module
npm install camera-remote-web-api
```

### Priority key is required

Before sending any camera commands (shooting, settings changes), you must set the priority key to `pc-remote`:

```typescript
await manager.client.setPriorityKey(cameraId, { setting: 'pc-remote' });
```

### Connection modes

- `remote` — Full camera control. Photos auto-transfer to host PC. Default mode.
- `remote-transfer` — Full camera control + explicit SD card file access. Most capable mode.
- `contents` — SD card file access only. No shooting or settings control.

### Complete minimal example

```typescript
import { CameraManager } from 'camera-remote-web-api/server';

const manager = new CameraManager({ port: 8080 });

manager.on('camera-ready', async ({ cameraId, camera }) => {
  console.log(`${camera.model} connected`);
  await manager.client.setPriorityKey(cameraId, { setting: 'pc-remote' });
  await manager.client.triggerShutter(cameraId);
  console.log('Photo captured!');
});

await manager.start();

// Graceful shutdown
process.on('SIGINT', async () => {
  await manager.close();
  process.exit(0);
});
```

## Documentation

- [Client SDK Overview](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/overview): Package overview, stateless vs stateful comparison
- [Quick Start](https://docs.crsdk.app/quickstarts/client-sdk): Install and capture a photo in under 5 minutes
- [Setup](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/reference): Installation, platform binaries, environment verification
- [CameraManager](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/camera-manager): Options, connection modes, lifecycle, camera access
- [Properties & Actions](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/properties-actions): Read/write camera settings, trigger shutter/focus/zoom
- [State Management](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/state): Camera state tracking, connection lifecycle events
- [Events](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/events): SSE event subscriptions — property changes, downloads, warnings
- [Examples](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/examples): Multi-camera, file transfer, live view, settings backup, continuous shooting
- [System Design](https://docs.crsdk.app/sdk/architecture): Browser vs server variants, framework patterns

## API Reference

- [HTTP API Overview](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/overview): RESTful endpoints, connection modes, response format
- [Properties](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/properties): Read/write camera properties (ISO, aperture, shutter speed)
- [Actions](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/actions): Shutter, focus, zoom control
- [Live View & OSD](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/live-view): Live view streaming, on-screen display overlay
- [Events (SSE)](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/events): Real-time camera callbacks via Server-Sent Events
- [File Transfer](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/sd-card): SD card file listing, download, save info configuration
- [Save & Recall Settings](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/settings): Camera settings file backup and restore
- [Compatibility](https://docs.crsdk.app/web-api/compatibility): Per-camera API support, connection modes, OS compatibility
